title: Designer round trip silently drops a bare FlowStep.value (condition expressions)
description:
DATA LOSS. fromFlowStep (app/renderer/components/workflow/flowStepMapping.ts) derives the node's value as:

    step.url ?? valueSource?.value ?? valueSource?.key ?? valueSource?.envKey ?? valueSource?.path ?? valueSource?.outputKey ?? ""

It NEVER reads step.value. So a FlowStep carrying only `value` (no `valueSource`, and not a goto) resolves to "" on load, and toFlowStep then emits `value: data.value || undefined` -> undefined. The value is destroyed by one designer open+save, with no error shown.

REACHABLE ON SHIPPED DATA: resources/test-fixtures/mock-site/flows/mock-conditional-flow.json stores its condition node with a `value` expression and no valueSource. Confirmed against the real converters:

    BEFORE  value = "<runtimeInputs.path expression>"   valueSource = undefined
    AFTER   value = undefined                           valueSource = undefined
    RESULT: condition expression LOST

Impact: opening such a flow in the Flow Designer and saving turns a working conditional into an empty one. A scan of resources/** found 69 steps with 1 currently affected. Externally authored or imported flows can hit this more broadly, since FlowStep.value is an independent optional field in the schema.

Found by scripts/verify-flow-step-mapping.mts during the Serialization Round-Trip Hardening phase. NOT introduced by the extraction: the moved functions are byte-identical to the originals (proven by diff). Pre-existing defect.

Current behavior is PINNED by two checks in that verifier under the "KNOWN DEFECT" section. When this is fixed, those two checks must be inverted.

Likely fix: either have fromFlowStep fall back to step.value when there is no url and no valueSource, OR require producers to always emit a static valueSource alongside value. Both are runtime behavior changes, so both were deliberately excluded from the hardening phase. Needs a decision on which side owns the invariant.
notes: FIXED (uncommitted, 2026-07-22) on feature/recorder-protected-login-and-async-awareness. Chose the designer-boundary fix: fromFlowStep reads step.value and marks a designer-only valueSourceType:'none' sentinel; createValueSource returns undefined for it so toFlowStep re-emits value alone WITHOUT fabricating a static valueSource. Inverted the 2 pinned KNOWN-DEFECT checks + added string/numeric/boolean/json/empty coverage. Same-class drops of generated(generator)+secret(secretName) found in the §8 pass and also fixed. verify:flow-step-mapping 94/0, tsc 0. Close on commit.

title: RT-05: edge identity regenerated from source/target instead of preserved
description:
createEdge sets id = edge-<source>-<target>, discarding the persisted edge id. Two connectors between the same pair of nodes - legal for conditional and parallel fan-out - collide onto one id after a load and are no longer distinguishable. Edge ids also appear verbatim in validateConnectorStructure diagnostics, so error messages stop pointing at a real connector.

Owner: app/renderer/components/workflow/flowProfileMapping.ts (createEdge).
Fix: thread the persisted edge.id through loadProfile; synthesize an id only for connectors created by user interaction.
Reproduce: npx tsx scripts/verify-random-roundtrip.mts (fixed seed awkit-roundtrip-baseline-001). Full diff, minimal failing definition and both original/reloaded definitions are in reports/random-tests/roundtrip-defects.json.
close_reason: Fixed in ab9f5f6 (Test Lab Tranche 1): the designer round-trip is now lossless; the RoundTripDefectCatalog entry was deleted so a recurrence reports as a regression. verify-random-roundtrip 26/0.

title: RT-01: locator discarded from steps not marked requiresLocator
description:
toFlowStep writes locator only when catalogItem.requiresLocator is true, so a saved locator on a screenshot or wait step is silently dropped.

Execution impact: wait with config.waitType=selector resolves step.locator (StepExecutor.ts:1524), so losing it breaks the wait; screenshot uses step.locator to capture an element (StepExecutor.ts:937), so losing it silently downgrades to a page screenshot and changes the stored artifact.

Owner: app/renderer/components/workflow/flowProfileMapping.ts.
Fix: persist locator whenever data.locatorValue is non-empty. requiresLocator is a VALIDATION rule (the user must supply one), not a PERSISTENCE rule; toFlowStep is the only place the two are conflated.
Reproduce: npx tsx scripts/verify-random-roundtrip.mts (fixed seed awkit-roundtrip-baseline-001). Full diff, minimal failing definition and both original/reloaded definitions are in reports/random-tests/roundtrip-defects.json.
close_reason: Fixed in ab9f5f6 (Test Lab Tranche 1): the designer round-trip is now lossless; the RoundTripDefectCatalog entry was deleted so a recurrence reports as a regression. verify-random-roundtrip 26/0.

title: RT-04: explicit step safety policy discarded on save
description:
FlowStep.safety is not mapped in either direction. It is authoritative for retry decisions when present; without it the runner falls back to node-type defaults and a keyword heuristic, so a step explicitly marked non-retryable (dangerousMutation) becomes retryable-by-heuristic after a designer save.

Owner: app/renderer/components/workflow/flowProfileMapping.ts.
Fix: pass safety through unchanged.
Reproduce: npx tsx scripts/verify-random-roundtrip.mts (fixed seed awkit-roundtrip-baseline-001). Full diff, minimal failing definition and both original/reloaded definitions are in reports/random-tests/roundtrip-defects.json.

title: RT-03: recorder popup/window metadata discarded on designer re-save
description:
toFlowStep never writes pageAlias/opensPopup/popupExpectation and fromFlowStep never reads them, so the Recorder metadata is lost on the first designer save.

Breaks the flow outright. The opener click arms popup capture only when step.opensPopup && step.popupExpectation (StepExecutor.ts:719); a later switchToPopup throws without a popupExpectation (StepExecutor.ts:764) and closePopup throws without an alias (StepExecutor.ts:801-802). A recorded multi-window flow works until the first designer save, then fails.

Owner: app/renderer/components/workflow/flowProfileMapping.ts.
Fix: carry the three fields through FlowDesignerNodeData as a pass-through; they are recorder-owned and need no UI.
Reproduce: npx tsx scripts/verify-random-roundtrip.mts (fixed seed awkit-roundtrip-baseline-001). Full diff, minimal failing definition and both original/reloaded definitions are in reports/random-tests/roundtrip-defects.json.
close_reason: Fixed in ab9f5f6 (Test Lab Tranche 1): the designer round-trip is now lossless; the RoundTripDefectCatalog entry was deleted so a recurrence reports as a regression. verify-random-roundtrip 26/0.

title: RT-02: secret-backed value sources are destroyed on save
description:
createValueSource has no secret branch and returns undefined when data.value is empty; fromFlowStep cannot recover a display value from secretName. A step with {type:secret, secretName:...} loses its whole value source on the first designer save - a flow that authenticated before the edit no longer does.

Note this is a LOSS, not a leak: no plaintext is written at any point. Verified with synthetic opaque references only; the lab never resolves a secret.

Owner: app/renderer/components/workflow/flowProfileMapping.ts.
Fix: add secretName to the recovery chain (into a dedicated field, not value) and give createValueSource a secret branch exempt from the empty-value guard.
Reproduce: npx tsx scripts/verify-random-roundtrip.mts (fixed seed awkit-roundtrip-baseline-001). Full diff, minimal failing definition and both original/reloaded definitions are in reports/random-tests/roundtrip-defects.json.
close_reason: Fixed by the same change as awkit-1w5 (RT-14 was its mechanism: createValueSource bailed on an empty data.value, and a secret carries a reference not a literal). 'secret references survive the round trip' now passes; no plaintext was involved at any point. Catalog entry deleted.

title: RT-14: value and valueSource flattened through one designer string, corrupting typed sources
description:
fromFlowStep collapses FlowStep.value and FlowStep.valueSource into a single data.value via: step.url ?? valueSource?.value ?? valueSource?.key ?? valueSource?.envKey ?? valueSource?.path ?? valueSource?.outputKey ?? "". createValueSource then rebuilds a typed source from that one string.

Most damaging round-trip finding. On a goto, step.url is FIRST in the chain and shadows the source entirely: {type:env, envKey:AWKIT_LAB_ENV_ALPHA} returns as {type:env, envKey:http://127.0.0.1:4321/form.html}, and {type:generated, generator:randomEmail} returns with a generator value outside its own union (a structurally invalid document). For other types, FlowStep.value is overwritten with the SOURCE KEY, destroying the literal fallback the runner would use. Sources whose discriminating field is not in the chain (secret, dynamic) collapse to empty and are dropped - this is also the mechanism behind RT-02.

Owner: app/renderer/components/workflow/flowProfileMapping.ts (fromFlowStep + createValueSource).
Fix: model the value source structurally in FlowDesignerNodeData instead of flattening. Immediate containment: remove step.url from the head of the recovery chain - it is the only entry that can silently overwrite an unrelated source field.
Reproduce: npx tsx scripts/verify-random-roundtrip.mts (fixed seed awkit-roundtrip-baseline-001). Full diff, minimal failing definition and both original/reloaded definitions are in reports/random-tests/roundtrip-defects.json.
close_reason: Fixed. valueSourceOriginal preserves the loaded source verbatim; createValueSource reconstructs only the two panel-authorable kinds (static/dynamic) and passes the rest through; data.value is the literal only; toFlowStep no longer persists url:''. Round-trip 8+15 -> 17 passed/12 failed, defect shapes 46 -> 35, 0 unexpected new failures. 8 new edit-path assertions added. verify:flow-designer 24/24 in real Electron.

title: Numeric record-to-replay fidelity gate for the recorder (measured %, not just pass/fail)
description:
Idea sourced from the 2026-07-22 browser-automation skills teardown
(BROWSER_AUTOMATION_SKILLS_REPORT.md sections 2A and 5). Reference implementation:
ruvnet/ruflo `browser-replay` + scripts/replay-spike.sh.

THE PATTERN WORTH STEALING: ruflo treats "is a recorded session actually replayable?" as
a MEASURED NUMBER, not a boolean. Their ADR-0001 will not move Proposed -> Accepted until
a spike demonstrates >=80% replay success across 10 distinct sites of varying DOM-drift
profiles. Per-run verdict = matched-steps / total-steps against a tolerance (default 0.85).
That converts a vague architectural bet into a ship gate.

CURRENT AWKIT STATE (verified):
- verify:recorder-flow tests recorded-actions -> flow SHAPE (Start/End nodes, wiring,
  waits as fixed-time, tab switches as Route Change). Pure logic, no browser.
- verify:recorder-locator, verify:recorder-draft, verify:flow-step-mapping cover locator
  generation, draft persistence, and round-trip serialization respectively.
- verify:runner does live checks against the mock site.
- What is MISSING: an end-to-end record -> replay loop that produces a FIDELITY
  PERCENTAGE across the mock-site scenario set. Today a replay either passes or fails a
  scenario; there is no aggregate number to watch for regression.

PROPOSED:
1. Harness that, for each applicable mock-site scenario, records a flow, replays it, and
   scores matched-steps / total-steps.
2. Emit an aggregate fidelity percentage plus a per-scenario table, so a drop is visible
   as a number rather than as one flipped assertion.
3. Pick a threshold and enforce it. ruflo chose 0.80 aggregate / 0.85 per-run; AWKIT's
   number should be set from a first baseline run, not copied.
4. Deliberately include drift-y scenarios so the metric has headroom to detect regression
   (the mock site is the Feature Test Lab - extend it per mock-site-maintainer).

WHY THIS MATTERS HERE: the randomized-test-lab round-trip work already proved that silent
fidelity loss is AWKIT's recurring failure mode (13 round-trip defects, awkit-cxa's bare
FlowStep.value drop). Those were each found by a targeted assertion after the fact. A
standing fidelity number would have shown the regression as a trend.

GOTCHAS FROM THE SOURCE MATERIAL:
- Network/timing nondeterminism produces false-fail verdicts. Pin to mock-site fixtures
  (AWKIT is offline-first, so this is easier here than upstream).
- If selector recovery needs more than one retry per step, log it - that is the signal
  the flow needs a RE-RECORD, not a replay.
- Do not let the aggregate hide a single catastrophic scenario; report both.

RELATED: awkit-wza (Randomized Automation Test Lab) - the campaign reporting phases
(awkit-wza.7) are the natural home for surfacing this number over time. Decide whether
this lands standalone or as part of that epic.
notes:
DESIGN WARNING from the v2 deep read of the reference implementation (BROWSER_AUTOMATION_SKILLS_REPORT.md section 3.3). ruflo's replay-spike.sh is the source of the >=80% gate idea AND a worked example of how to get it wrong. Three defects to avoid when building the AWKIT version:

1. Its recorded baseline is open -> snapshot -> close. No click, no fill, no navigation. There is nothing whose selector could drift, so the metric cannot observe the phenomenon it claims to measure. AWKIT's baseline scenarios MUST contain real interactions.

2. Its scoring is dead code:
     local orig_steps=2
     local replay_steps=2
     if [[ -f "$RESULTS_DIR/$label.replay.snap.json" ]] && [[ -s ... ]]; then echo PASS
   Both counters are hardcoded and never compared. The real pass condition is 'the snapshot file is non-empty'. Score matched-steps/total-steps for real, or do not publish a percentage.

3. The documented per-run tolerance (0.85) and the embedding-similarity retry are unimplemented in both the harness and the MCP tool.

Net: the gate measures 'can we open a URL twice'. Before shipping the AWKIT metric, answer explicitly: what regression turns this number red? If there is no realistic answer, the metric is decorative.

title: Async engine: grouped completion composition (A AND (B OR C))
description:
Completion policy is a single per-step scalar (FlowStep.completionMode) applied to ALL afterWaits, giving only flat AND (allRequired) or flat OR (anyRequired). Nested/grouped composition is not expressible.

CONCRETE GAP (found during the Electron GUI gate pre-flight, 2026-07-22): the empty-result contract

    API success AND (tableHasRows OR emptyStateVisible)

cannot be configured. resolveAnyRequired (src/runner/StepExecutor.ts) runs Promise.any across EVERY required condition INCLUDING the armed API response, so the API resolving first satisfies the whole step and neither UI outcome is ever required. That violates the rule 'a successful API status alone must not override a missing required UI outcome'. networkThenUi does not help: phase 3 requires ALL required UI outcomes (AND), so the two UI branches still cannot be OR-ed. Marking both optional means neither is required and the step passes with neither present.

NOT a regression - nothing previously worked and broke. This is missing expressiveness.

Likely shape: an explicit one-of / any-of GROUP inside afterWaits (e.g. a group condition holding N child conditions with its own required/optional flag), so allRequired can contain an OR group. Must EXTEND the existing WaitCondition union rather than fork a parallel model (see the 13 round-trip defect history), and must round-trip through toFlowStep/fromFlowStep + the JSON store.

Blocked behind: Serialization Round-Trip Hardening (extraction + executable round-trip verifier) so the new nested shape is covered by round-trip tests from day one. Also gates Electron GUI check 11 configuration 3.
notes: IMPLEMENTED (uncommitted, 2026-07-22). Extended the WaitCondition union (NOT a fork) with an anyOf OR-group: passes when ANY child passes, fails only when all fail; as one required condition under allRequired it expresses 'API success AND (tableHasRows OR emptyStateVisible)'. Runner resolves via Promise.any in executeWaitCondition (works under every completion policy); FlowValidation.clampWaits recurses; asyncCompletionReview.reviewWait rolls up worst branch. Designer: renderWaitEditor refactored to (wait,update) for recursive nested branches + '+ OR group' button + token .anyof-* CSS. verify:waits 52/0 (incl. 'API ok but neither branch -> fails'), round-trip in verify:flow-step-mapping. /async-results fixture already exposes #resultsTable + empty-state, so GUI check 11.3 is configurable; manual walkthrough remains. Close on commit + GUI 11.3 PASS.

title: 9 of 13 controlled defect classes are rejected by no validator
description:
Phase 2 mutation testing found that these controlled defects pass every validator in src/: missingRequiredValue, invalidConnectorTarget, unsupportedOperator, duplicateNodeId, missingEndNode, unreachableNode, invalidLoopLimit, invalidTimeout, missingFlowReference.

Common cause: several of these rules DO exist, but only in the renderer advisory validateFlow (FlowChartDesigner.tsx), which is not the save gate - connectorStructureIssues is - and has no counterpart in src/. So a profile that reaches the runner by import, hand edit or IPC is never checked.

Worst of them: unreachableNode. There is NO reachability check anywhere at the flow level, only per-node degree. A step the author believes runs may silently never run, with nothing reporting it. verify-random-generator.mts already implements the forward BFS and it can be lifted into src/.

Also notable: JsonProfileStore parses with an unvalidated cast (ProfileStore.ts:145,174), which is why unsupportedOperator survives - connector config literals are enforced by TypeScript at author time only.

Each gap is catalogued with citation, runtime risk and a recommended fix in src/testing/oracle/TestExecutionOracle.ts (MUTATION_EXPECTATIONS) and reports/random-tests/validation-gaps.md. The oracle asserts each is STILL a gap, so closing one or opening a new one both fail the verifier.
Reproduce: npx tsx scripts/verify-random-oracle.mts
notes: 2026-07-21 Stage 2a: all 9 defect classes are now DETECTED by src/validation/FlowValidator.ts and verify-random-oracle is green (26/0, was 19/1). Bead stays OPEN: the engine is wired into NO production caller, so the run gate/save/import still let these through. Closing requires Stage 2b (PreRunValidator delegates; designer/builder show issues). Checklist: TestExecutionOracle.PRODUCTION_UNENFORCED_RULES.
close_reason: Stage 2b: PreRunValidator delegates all flow-definition rules to the shared FlowValidator engine, so the run gate rejects every one of the 9 defect classes when execution-blocking (active-path errors + connector-structure errors). Off-path errors (unreachableNode) are reported non-blocking by design. verify-random-oracle 27/0 asserts detected AND production-enforced for all 13 kinds.

Bead stays OPEN: PreRunValidator.ts:55 still hardcodes its drifted list. The oracle now asserts the production drift is exactly [radio] so it cannot change unnoticed. Fix lands in Stage 2b.

title: branding-splash broke firstWindow()-based Electron GUI verifiers
description: The branding-splash feature (PR #12) shows a splash BrowserWindow first, so Playwright's app.firstWindow() now returns the splash — which has no window.playwrightFlowStudio preload bridge. Every GUI verifier that does app.firstWindow() then win.evaluate(() => window.playwrightFlowStudio...) throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading settings)'. Confirmed on verify:settings-persistence (fixed in the oracle epic via a resolveMainWindow helper that polls app.windows() for the bridge). Likely also affects verify:capacity-settings-gui, verify:instance-monitor-gui, verify:runtime-analytics-gui, verify:workflow-builder, verify:flow-designer, verify:reports (all use _electron + firstWindow). Fix: add a shared resolveMainWindow(app) helper (see scripts/verify-oracle-drivers-gui.mjs / verify-settings-persistence.mjs) that waits for the window exposing window.playwrightFlowStudio.settings, and use it instead of firstWindow(). NOT an Oracle-refactor defect.
notes: EXPANDED ROOT CAUSE (2026-07-18 audit): the splash is only HALF the breakage. PR #15 (Secure Login) added SecurityGate to main.tsx, which mounts ONLY sign-in surfaces until auth — the real <App/> shell (and all nav-items/routes) never mounts pre-auth. So every app-shell GUI verifier needs BOTH fixes: (1) resolveMainWindow(app) polling app.windows() for window.playwrightFlowStudio.settings (splash has no bridge), AND (2) isolate LOCALAPPDATA to a fresh temp dir + drive first-run (provision Super User -> auto sign-in) to reach the shell. title: Locator recovery memory: remember the winning candidate, recover when all recorded candidates miss
description:
Idea sourced from the 2026-07-22 browser-automation skills teardown
(BROWSER_AUTOMATION_SKILLS_REPORT.md sections 2A and 5). Reference implementation:
ruvnet/ruflo AgentDB `browser-selectors` namespace, keyed <host>:<intent> ->
{selector, ref, snapshot-hash, last-success}, recovered at replay time by embedding
similarity.

CURRENT AWKIT STATE (verified in src/runner/LocatorFactory.ts resolve()):
AWKIT already does the hard part, and arguably better than the reference:
  1. container/frame context so candidates resolve inside the right subtree
  2. primary candidate, then `alternatives` in recorded order
  3. unique match wins; else the single VISIBLE match wins; else single ENABLED;
     else single IN-VIEWPORT; never guesses between equally-actionable twins
  4. all-counts-zero returns the primary so the caller's action auto-waits
  5. genuinely ambiguous -> clear diagnostic via formatFailure
Alternatives are carried on the node (recorderInitScript.buildAlternatives) rather than
looked up at replay time.

THE ACTUAL GAP - two things the recorded-alternatives design cannot do:
(a) NO RECOVERY WHEN EVERY RECORDED CANDIDATE MISSES. Once the primary and all
    alternatives resolve to zero, the step fails. There is no similarity fallback to a
    structurally-near element.
(b) NO LEARNING. Which candidate actually won last run is discarded. A flow whose primary
    has been dead for months still pays the full miss-then-fallback cost every run, and
    nothing records that the site drifted.

PROPOSED (incremental, offline - no embeddings service, no network):
1. Persist last-success per step: which candidate index resolved, plus a cheap structural
   fingerprint of the matched element. Reorder candidates by last-success on the next run.
   This is (b) alone and is low-risk.
2. Only if (1) proves insufficient: a local similarity fallback when all candidates miss -
   score visible elements against the recorded fingerprint (role/name/text/attrs/ancestry)
   and accept a match only above a high threshold, logging loudly that recovery fired.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- Offline-first: no embedding model, no remote vector store. Structural/lexical similarity
  computed locally, or nothing.
- Preserve the existing invariant from LocatorFactory: recovery may only convert
  would-be FAILURES into successes. It must never change which element an
  already-unambiguous step resolves to. Any similarity path must run strictly AFTER the
  all-candidates-missed branch.
- Never emit or match on utility/hashed classes (established recorder rule).
- Recovery firing is a signal the flow needs a re-record. Surface it, do not silently heal
  forever.

VERIFY: verify:recorder-locator for candidate ordering and fingerprinting (pure logic);
verify:runner for the live miss-then-recover path. Feeds the fidelity metric in awkit-60w.

title: Instance Monitor: second read-only CDP observation client + on-disk run trace
description:
Idea sourced from the 2026-07-22 browser-automation skills teardown
(BROWSER_AUTOMATION_SKILLS_REPORT.md sections 2E and 5). Reference implementation:
Browserbase `skills/browser-trace`.

KEYSTONE FACT: every Chrome DevTools target accepts MULTIPLE concurrent CDP clients.
So an observation client can attach to a target that the runner is already driving,
enable only observation domains (Network, Console, Runtime, Log, Page), and never send
action commands. Non-intrusive by construction.

CURRENT AWKIT GAP (verified): no surface attaches a CDP client for observation.
src/session/SessionCaptureService.ts is explicitly "No CDP connection"; the
https-certificate-trust work confirmed AWKIT has only two context factories and no
CDP attach path. Instance Monitor (app/renderer/pages/InstanceMonitor.tsx,
verify:instance-monitor + verify:instance-monitor-gui) shows run state but has no
event firehose or live browser view.

PROPOSED (offline-safe, all local):
1. Observation-only CDP client attached to a running instance's Chromium. Never issues
   actions. Fails open - if attach fails the run is unaffected.
2. On-disk run trace under the existing artifacts root (NOT resources/, NOT app.asar),
   modelled on Browserbase's .o11y/<run-id>/ layout:
     manifest.json          run metadata, target, domains, started/stopped
     index.jsonl            one line per sample {ts, screenshot, dom, url}
     cdp/raw.ndjson         firehose, one JSON object per line
     cdp/summary.json       session totals + per-page domains/network/timing
     cdp/<bucket>/*.jsonl   network / console / runtime / log / page buckets
     cdp/pages/<pid>/       per-page slices, bisected on top-level Page.frameNavigated
     screenshots/<ts>.png, dom/<ts>.html
   summary.json is the analysis entry point.
3. Live browser viewing in Instance Monitor built on the same attach.

WHY IT FITS AWKIT: the layout is a filesystem tree with zero network dependency, so it
survives the offline-first constraint that rules out the cloud live_url / debugger_url
approach that browser-use and Browserbase use.

SCOPE NOTE: distinct from awkit-4km, which covers CDP diagnostics for *wait/async
resolution* (redirect chains, timing, request ids) inside the runner. This bead is the
*observability* surface: a passive tracer plus its persisted artifact plus the monitor UI.
Coordinate so both share one attach helper rather than opening two clients.

GOTCHAS FROM THE SOURCE MATERIAL:
- Do not poll screenshots/DOM faster than ~1s; 2s is the recommended default.
- Adding the DOM domain is very noisy - opt-in only.
- Always run the stop path even after a crash, or background samplers linger.
- rrweb-style DOM replay is deprecated upstream; screenshot + DOM timeline is the
  accepted visual ground truth. Do not build on rrweb.
- Retention/size policy needed: raw.ndjson grows fast. Reuse the artifacts retention
  path (verify:artifacts).

VERIFY: extend verify:instance-monitor for the trace shape and bisection, and
verify:artifacts for retention. Bisection is pure logic and can be tested without a browser.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS harvested from the reference (browserbase/skills browser-trace scripts/bisect-cdp.mjs + lib.mjs, ~300 lines, Node stdlib only). These are the non-obvious correctness points that would otherwise cost real debugging time:

1. TWO CDP CLOCKS. Network/Page emit MonotonicTime (seconds since browser start, small). Console.messageAdded emits TimeSinceEpoch (ms, > 1e9). Anchor wall-clock conversion ONLY on monotonic timestamps:
     const isMonotonic = ts => ts != null && ts < 1e9;
   Mixing them silently corrupts every derived duration.

2. Runtime.consoleAPICalled MUST be remapped into the Console domain bucket. Their comment: without the remap the Console bucket's errors/warnings counts 'would never line up with any entry in the counts map and would silently disappear from the per-page summary'.

3. Events arriving BEFORE the first navigation clamp to page 0 (not -1), because those requests really are part of loading the first page.

4. Top-frame detection: Page.frameNavigated with params.frame.parentId null OR empty string. Without the parentId check, iframe navigations create spurious page boundaries.

5. Bisect is a SEPARATE IDEMPOTENT PASS over raw.ndjson, not inline during capture. rmSync the per-page tree and rebuild. Cheap to re-run when the bucket map changes.

6. Session-wide buckets always written (even empty) for predictable shape; per-page buckets use skipEmpty and delete the file when empty.

7. Bucket map is 17 predicates: network/{requests,responses,finished,failed,websocket}, console/{logs,exceptions}, runtime/all, log/entries, page/{navigations,lifecycle,dialogs,frames,all}, dom/all, target/{attached,detached}.

8. summary.json is the analysis ENTRY POINT (session totals + pages[] with per-page domain counts, network.byType, timing) so a reader opens one small file before drilling in.

Also worth copying: autobrowse's unify-trace.mjs joins the agent's intent log and the CDP firehose into ONE wall-clock-ordered NDJSON tagged source:'agent'|'browser'. For AWKIT that is runner step log + CDP events interleaved — the single highest-value debugging artifact in the whole survey.
